questions.question: What does multimodal RAG require beyond the solved single-modality pattern?
options: Just a bigger embedding model

Multiple retrieval heads (compatible embedding spaces per modality), fusion of results across modalities, generation grounding that cites mixed-modality sources, and evaluation metrics covering cross-modal signal
A relational database instead of vectors
Nothing — CLIP already solves it all
questions.explanation: The three 2025 surveys (Abootorabi, Mei, Zhao et al.) independently arrive at this same four-part taxonomy of sub-problems.

questions.question: What are the three cross-modal retrieval patterns?
options: Keyword, fuzzy, and semantic search

Shared embedding space (CLIP/CLAP, cosine works directly but limited to trained pairs), per-modality encoders + a translator module (flexible, more complex), and VLM hidden states as the representation (any supported modality, higher quality, more expensive)
SQL joins across modality tables
Always converting everything to text first
questions.explanation: Practical choice: CLIP/SigLIP 2 for text+image, CLAP for text+audio, VLM hidden states when frontier quality justifies the cost.

questions.question: You retrieved 5 images, 3 text passages, and 2 audio clips. What is the production-default fusion?
options: Discard everything but the top modality
Score fusion: normalize scores within each modality, then sum — with a slight bias toward the query's dominant modality; upgrade to MoE fusion only if A/B testing shows clear domain wins
Always train an attention fusion network first
Random shuffling of all ten results
questions.explanation: Score fusion is cheapest and often works. Attention-based fusion needs training; MoE fusion routes query types to modality experts (visual questions weight images higher) — worth it only when proven.

questions.question: What does generation grounding look like with mixed-modality sources?
options: Citations are impossible across modalities
Each claim cites the item that drove it, in modality-appropriate form: [1] for text, [img 3] with a short caption for images, [audio 2 at 0:34] with a timestamp for audio
One blanket citation at the end
Only text sources can be cited
questions.explanation: "Cite your sources" must survive the modality mix — timestamps for audio, captions for images — or the user can't verify which retrieved evidence supports which claim.

questions.question: Which real workflows motivate going wider than document RAG?
options: Only academic benchmark chasing
Trip planning ("quiet vegan brunch with natural light"), medical triage (photo + notes), e-commerce ("outfits like this selfie, in my size"), and field service (engine sound + part photo)
Spreadsheet formula generation
Password recovery flows
questions.explanation: Each pairs a query in one modality with evidence in others — exactly the cross-modal retrieval + fusion + grounded generation stack this lesson designs.
